nodes.label: AI as time-saving example generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as error-prone content creator requiring extensive vetting
nodes.grounding_span: I use it a lot to create examples. It saves time, when it's correct

nodes.label: AI output quality and specificity
nodes.label_negative: AI output with repetition, errors, or inappropriate difficulty
nodes.grounding_span: if you can be specific enough and spend some time crafting good prompts, you can get some good help

nodes.label: AI reliability for pedagogical alignment
nodes.label_negative: AI unreliability for learning outcomes coverage and distractor quality
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I'd trust any model (yet) to be able to do that

nodes.label: AI formatting and integration friction
nodes.grounding_span: I cannot deal with AI formatting, so I'm going back and forth to Word

nodes.label: AI response speed relative to quality
nodes.label_negative: AI fast response with high quality
nodes.grounding_span: models that are taking 2 or 3 minutes now to think before responding...I don't think the new responses are all that much better

nodes.label: AI impact on student motivation and learning
nodes.label_negative: AI as tool that enhances student engagement
nodes.grounding_span: Motivating them when AI is so readily available is difficult

nodes.label: Pedagogical quality and accuracy
nodes.grounding_span: I still have to go through every example with a fine tooth comb to be sure that it is correct

nodes.label: Professional autonomy and judgment
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think I'd trust any model (yet) to be able to do that

nodes.label: Student engagement and love for learning
nodes.grounding_span: I love my job and I love math

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for initial content generation
nodes.grounding_span: I was happily shocked the first time I went looking for integration problems and got a decent list from AI

nodes.label: Frustration with AI output quality and speed
nodes.grounding_span: when there's some repetition or some nuance that didn't translate...it can be frustrating

nodes.label: Resignation about AI's impact on student motivation
nodes.grounding_span: Lots of students don't bother to do their own work anymore, which is really depressing

nodes.label: Openness to future AI use for simulations
nodes.grounding_span: I'll probably start using AI to show simulations of adding the area under a curve

nodes.label: Iterative prompt refinement and quality control
nodes.grounding_span: if you can be specific enough and spend some time crafting good prompts

nodes.label: Systematic pedagogical curation and editing
nodes.grounding_span: I take the questions and look through to see what I can use first
